principle: Tamagui styles are a superset of React Native styles
explanation: Base style props start from ReactNative ViewStyle/TextStyle and add: cross-platform shadow/filter/backgroundImage/border/outline, web-only CSS features, flat transform props (x, y, scale, rotate‚Ä¶), pseudo states, enter/exit, and parent-based selectors. Works via inline props on View/Text and inside `styled()`. Exported types: ViewStyle, TextStyle.

principle: styled(Component, config) creates an optimizable component
explanation: Extends any Tamagui component with custom styles: `styled(View, { borderRadius: 20, variants: {...} })`. Gives you typed props, compiler-friendly style shape, and variant merging. `styled(styled())` still yields a single React component in the tree ‚Äî variants and nested styling compose without adding render depth. Don't wrap Pressable/TouchableOpacity ‚Äî their event logic conflicts.

principle: Inline styles are first-class ‚Äî the compiler flattens them
explanation: Tamagui encourages inline style props: `<View mx="$sm" scale={1.2}><Text c="$color" /></View>`. With shorthands it is concise and the optimizing compiler hoists/extracts these into CSS at build time ‚Äî no runtime cost. You don't have to choose between inline and `styled()`.

principle: Prop order matters: later props override earlier ones
explanation: `<View background="red" {...props} width={200} />` means background is default-overridable by props, but width is locked to 200. Same for variants: `<Square scale={3} huge />` applies huge last, so huge wins; `<Square huge scale={3} />` applies scale last. Making order meaningful is what lets variants expand to multiple properties without boilerplate.

principle: Flat transform props: x, y, scale, rotate ‚Äî no transform array
explanation: Instead of `transform: [{translateX: 10}]` you write `x={10} y={20} scale={1.1} rotate="45deg"`. Supported: x, y, perspective, scale, scaleX, scaleY, skewX, skewY, matrix, rotate, rotateX, rotateY, rotateZ. These compile to the equivalent transform entries. Cleaner ergonomics and animation targets.

principle: Pseudo style props: hoverStyle, pressStyle, focusStyle, disabledStyle
explanation: Inline pseudo-state objects: `hoverStyle` (web, :hover), `pressStyle` (while pressed), `focusStyle` (:focus), `focusVisibleStyle` (:focus-visible, keyboard), `focusWithinStyle` (:focus-within on descendants), `disabledStyle` (disabled prop true). Each accepts any Tamagui style props. Compiles to atomic CSS on web; runtime on native.

principle: enterStyle and exitStyle drive mount/unmount animations
explanation: `enterStyle` = initial styles on mount (animate from). `exitStyle` = target styles on unmount (animate to). Tamagui normalizes opacity/scale to 1 and y to 0 on the base when only defined in enter/exitStyle. Use with `<AnimatePresence>` for unmount; direct children need unique `key` props.

principle: $-prefix selectors target parent state: media, theme, platform, group
explanation: Use `$` on style keys to apply nested style objects conditionally: `$sm`/`$gtMd` (media queries), `$theme-dark` (top-level theme), `$platform-ios`/`$platform-native` (platform), `$group-*` (parent group state). Each accepts the full style-prop set. Works on native and web through CSS or runtime.

principle: Media queries live in createTamagui({ media }), no prefix at use
explanation: Define `createTamagui({ media: { sm: { maxWidth: 800 } } })`. Use as `<Text $sm={{ color: 'blue' }} />`. V5 renames 2xl‚Üíxxl; max queries use kebab-case (`max-xxl`). Height queries like `height-sm`, `height-md`, `height-lg`, plus `touch` and `hoverable` for device capabilities.

principle: useMedia() is a signal-like hook ‚Äî only subscribes to accessed keys
explanation: `const media = useMedia()` returns a proxy that tracks which keys you access; re-renders only when those keys change. The compiler can fully extract usages and remove the hook from output. Proxy constraints: not iterable, no `in`/Object.keys, no destructuring with renaming. Access keys directly like `media.sm` for best compiler support.

principle: useTheme proxies the current theme with per-key tracking
explanation: `const theme = useTheme()` returns the current theme resolved up through parents. Values are Variable objects with `.val`, `.variable`, `.name`. On web, `theme.bg.get()` returns the CSS variable reference (no re-render on theme change); on native it returns the value and re-renders. Pass component/theme names as args to target a specific sub-theme.

principle: Groups: name a parent, style children based on its state or size
explanation: Set `group` (unnamed) or `group="header"` (named) on a parent. Children target it via `$group-hover`, `$group-press`, `$group-focus` (pseudo), `$group-sm` (container query), or named variants like `$group-header-hover`. Container queries use the same breakpoints from `createTamagui({ media })`. Type named groups with `interface TypeOverride { groupNames(): 'card' | 'header' }`.

principle: Group containers on native need untilMeasured or fixed dims
explanation: Native can't know a component's layout before first render. Add `untilMeasured="hide"` on the parent to start invisible until measured, preventing flicker. Alternatively set explicit width/height on the container so children can match a container query on first paint and avoid the double render altogether.

principle: Cross-platform CSS shorthand with $variables in strings
explanation: `boxShadow`, `backgroundImage`, `filter`, `border`, `outline` accept string shorthand with token refs: `boxShadow="0 0 10px $shadowColor"`, `backgroundImage="linear-gradient(to bottom, $background, $color)"`, `filter="blur($2)"`, `border="1px solid $borderColor"`. Web: emits CSS vars. Native: expands to individual props (borderWidth/Style/Color). url() in backgroundImage is web-only.

principle: rem units work cross-platform with PixelRatio.getFontScale()
explanation: Write `<View padding="1rem" />` ‚Äî on web browsers handle rem; on native Tamagui multiplies by `PixelRatio.getFontScale()` so the user's accessibility font setting scales everything. Configure base with `createTamagui({ settings: { remBaseFontSize: 16 } })`.

principle: Variants are the only sanctioned way to share conditional styles
explanation: Variants live inside `styled()` and receive a value + (tokens, theme, props, font, context). They run outside the render function ‚Äî no hooks, clear contract, type-safe, compiler-friendly. They nest without adding tree depth. Use `as const` on the variant object until TS can infer constant objects. Alternative (free-form logic in a component) de-opts the compiler.

principle: Typed variant keys: true/false, :string, :boolean, :number
explanation: `true`/`false` create a boolean prop. `:string`, `:boolean`, `:number` are type hints ‚Äî the variant function receives the typed value. `true`/`false` take precedence over `:boolean`. Example: `color: { ':string': (c) => ({ color: c, borderColor: c }) }` accepts any string color.

principle: Spread variants gather values from a token category
explanation: Instead of enumerating every size, write `pad: { '...size': (val, { tokens }) => ({ padding: tokens.size[val] }) }`. The `...` prefix is required ‚Äî it types the prop against that token category and removes per-key boilerplate. Allowed: ...color, ...size, ...space, ...font, ...fontSize, ...lineHeight, ...radius, ...letterSpace, ...zIndex.

principle: Catch-all variant: '...' handles any value not matched by others
explanation: Mix typed cases with a fallback: `colorful: { true: { color: 'red' }, '...': (val: string) => ({ color: val }) }`. The catch-all runs only when the value doesn't match named keys. Useful for extending a preset set with arbitrary values.

principle: Dynamic variants: a single function instead of object syntax
explanation: For maximum flexibility: `variants: { doubleMargin: (val: number) => ({ margin: val * 2 }) }`. Shorter than the object-with-keys form but less compiler-optimizable than typed spread variants. Use when types are complex or the calculation is essential.

principle: defaultVariants sets typed defaults for variants
explanation: TS can't infer variant types on the object they're defined on (left-to-right parsing). Put defaults in the separate `defaultVariants` key ‚Äî properly typed: `defaultVariants: { size: '$10' }`. The component renders with those values when the prop is omitted.

principle: Variants can include pseudo and media styles
explanation: Inside a variant value you can nest media-query and pseudo blocks: `size: { md: { fontSize: '$sm', $gtMd: { fontSize: '$md' }, hoverStyle: {‚Ä¶} } }`. Variants have the full power of Tamagui's style system.

principle: Child variants can trigger parent variants via composition
explanation: `styled(ParentStyled, { variants: { hero: { true: { large: true } } } })` ‚Äî setting hero on the child activates the parent's `large` variant. Variant values can reference parent variants directly, composing styles without duplication.

principle: Functional variants receive a utility bag as 2nd arg
explanation: Second argument: `{ theme, tokens, props, font, fontFamily, fonts, context }`. `theme` is a proxy with `$`-prefixed keys; `tokens` has all categories; `props` are the component's current props; `font` is the resolved font object (may be undefined); `context` is the current styledContext for compound components.

principle: createStyledContext shares variant props across compound components
explanation: For compound APIs like `<Button.Icon>`/`<Button.Text>`, `createStyledContext()` scopes shared variants (e.g. `size`) across siblings. Pass the context via `context: ButtonContext` on each `styled()`; wrap with `<Button.Props size="$large">` to set from above. Caveat: using context currently disables compiler flattening ‚Äî keep it off hot primitives like Stack/Text.

principle: withStaticProperties attaches subcomponents to a parent
explanation: `withStaticProperties(ButtonFrame, { Text: ButtonText, Icon: ButtonIcon, Props: Context.Provider })` returns one component you can use as `<Button.Text>` / `<Button.Icon>` / `<Button.Props>`. Standard compound component pattern that keeps import surface small and makes relationships visible in JSX.
